briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon, everyone. I will now begin the climate briefing for May 12, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Climate Crisis Warnings and Worsening Inequality
sections.content: Recent European research has warned that climate change is exacerbating health inequalities, leading to 100,000 deaths. These costs of inequality demonstrate the widespread impact of climate change across society [3, 6]. Furthermore, analyses suggest that drought surpassing a critical threshold could heighten the risk of war, and reports indicate that the climate crisis is fueling a 'catastrophic' increase in malaria outbreaks [8, 41]. Scientists are warning that even slow-moving climate change can lead to sudden species collapse, and that California wildlife's evolutionary capacity cannot keep pace with the rate of climate change [45, 47]. Concerns have also been raised that 2026, in particular, could be an extreme year as the global warming agenda is pushed aside [54].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Strengthening Domestic Climate Adaptation and Disaster Preparedness
sections.content: Domestically, news continues regarding adaptation efforts to upcoming summer climate change and strengthened disaster preparedness. The Ministry of Climate has reviewed summer flood response measures [2], and the Korea Meteorological Administration (KMA) announced its 2026 summer disaster prevention weather measures, reorganizing the heatwave advisory system after 18 years and introducing a new 'Severe Heatwave Warning' [7, 9]. Meanwhile, KAIST has developed an 'Integrated Climate Research Model' capable of simultaneously analyzing the socio-economic impacts of climate change, thereby establishing a more sophisticated response framework [1]. Changes are also being observed in daily life, with the retail industry, for example, seeing GS25 developing 'all-weather umbrellas' as a strategic product in response to climate change [4]. These efforts reflect the concerns of the majority of the public regarding climate change and are serving to raise social awareness about changes in daily life and identity [44, 49].

sections.title: 🌱 Domestic and International Efforts for Carbon Neutrality
sections.content: Various domestic and international efforts are actively underway to achieve carbon neutrality. Domestically, Nowon-gu has opened a 'Carbon Neutrality Experience Center,' establishing a hub for eco-climate education [5], and carbon neutrality education and experiential activities have been successfully completed at various educational institutions, including Seosan Seoil High School [12, 21, 22]. Gwangmyeong City is supporting scientific policy formulation by establishing a 'Data Station' and an 'Integrated Platform' that integrate carbon neutrality data across transportation, environment, and energy sectors [11, 14, 19]. Furthermore, Incheon TP held a pledge ceremony with green climate industry companies to participate in achieving carbon neutrality [15, 17], and Chungnam Province is encouraging public participation by operating 'Carbon Up-Shoe,' which provides incentives for carbon neutrality practices [16]. Internationally, a Siemens factory achieved carbon neutrality with a solar microgrid, and there was also a proposal to make Malta carbon neutral by 2050 [63, 64].

sections.title: 🌱 Accelerating Renewable Energy Transition: Policy and Practice
sections.content: Policy proposals and on-site application efforts to accelerate the transition to renewable energy are spreading nationwide. The National Assembly Futures Institute has proposed the establishment of the 1st Basic Plan for Renewable Energy [28], and Jeollanam-do Province is taking proactive steps, such as requesting full support for renewable energy projects and securing national funding for resident-participatory renewable energy models from the Ministry of Planning [23, 29, 30, 31, 34, 35, 38]. Uijeongbu City has fully launched its renewable energy expansion policy, aiming to increase its electricity self-sufficiency rate from 1% to 19% [24, 26, 32]. A renewable energy transition roadmap has also been established for the agriculture and rural sectors [25], and corporate participation is expanding, with KT, for example, supplying renewable energy directly to Tokyo Electron Korea via a PPA scheme [27]. These efforts emphasize the importance of establishing public standards for the coexistence of offshore wind power and fisheries, and of on-site participation [10].

sections.title: 🌍 International Climate Science and Policy Trends
sections.content: Internationally, discussions are active on various aspects of climate change and policy challenges. Scientific findings that microplastics can float in the atmosphere, absorb heat, and contribute to global warming are raising new concerns [46, 57]. Furthermore, research results have been published indicating that climate change is altering rivers, daily life, and identity, and that ocean oxygen depletion affects metabolic processes [49, 50]. Meanwhile, in-depth research on specific impacts continues, with hypotheses such as climate change triggering large-scale agricultural pest populations being refuted by scientists [42]. In terms of policy, the Paris Agreement Committee was criticized for omitting climate plans for Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs) [53], and the Holy See warned that war and climate change are having a devastating impact on food security [58, 60]. The Ghanaian government is urging international cooperation, emphasizing joint action and investment in climate change [61].

briefing.closing: As we have seen in today's briefing, the threat of climate change is growing, but simultaneously, diverse domestic and international efforts to address it are continuing. Continued attention and action are crucial. This concludes today's climate briefing.

articles.description: The Korean automotive industry marks its 50th anniversary of exports this year, achieving a cumulative total of 76.55 million units since the first 6 Hyundai Pony cars were exported in 1976. While cumulative exports are expected to exceed 80 million next year, the industry faces challenges from a temporary slowdown (chasm) in the global EV market and competition from China.

summary.phenomenon: The Korean automotive industry, celebrating its 50th anniversary of exports this year, set a major record by achieving cumulative exports of 76.55 million units, starting with the export of 6 Hyundai Pony cars in 1976.
summary.outlook: While cumulative exports are expected to surpass 80 million units next year, the industry is experiencing a slowdown due to the temporary demand stagnation (chasm) in the global electric vehicle market and China's aggressive competition.
